title: JWT Decoder
metaDescription: Decode JWT (JSON Web Token) headers and payloads instantly in your browser. Signature is not verified — this is a decoding tool, not a validator.
shortDescription: Decode a JWT's header and payload. Does not verify the signature.

guideSections.heading: Decoding a Token Tells You Nothing About Whether It's Valid
guideSections.body: A JWT's header and payload are only base64URL-encoded, not encrypted, which means anyone can decode and read them without any key. Decoding confirms what a token claims, not whether those claims are genuine — verifying authenticity requires checking the signature against the issuer's secret or public key, which this tool deliberately does not do since that key is never available client-side.

guideSections.heading: Why JWTs Use Base64URL Instead of Standard Base64
guideSections.body: Standard base64 uses +, /, and = characters, all of which have special meaning inside a URL. JWTs are frequently passed as URL parameters or HTTP headers, so the spec swaps in URL-safe characters and drops padding — which is why naively running standard base64 decoding on a raw JWT segment often fails.
